title: Call for Event Day Staff
content: We are recruiting event day staff from now until the end of June. Please see the registration form for details.

title: Donation Rewards
content:
iPlayground 2026 exclusive merchandise is here!

This year, we have prepared exclusive omamori charms, cold water bottles, two styles of decorative magnets, limited commemorative graphic T-shirts, and sports towels, bringing this year’s key visual and “Code a Future, Build the Amazing” into your everyday life. Whether you want to collect memories from the event, support the community, or pick up something practical to accompany you at work, during workouts, or at conferences, these items are a great choice. This year’s merchandise can be picked up on-site at the event, or you may purchase an additional delivery service to have it shipped to you after the event.

Pre-orders close on July 15. Orders placed after the deadline may not be available for on-site pickup and will instead be shipped after the event. A limited quantity of items will also be available on-site, but supplies are limited, so we recommend pre-ordering early. Donation receipts will be issued for all donations and can be collected at the finance counter after event check-in.

buttons.title: It Just Turned into Something You Love

title: Announcing Our International Guest Speakers
content:
Hello everyone, and thank you for supporting iPlayground last year. Thanks to a surplus in last year's event budget, we've decided to invest all of those remaining resources into inviting international speakers for iPlayground 2026. These speakers were personally selected by our team after attending conferences around the world and discovering some of the most inspiring and highly regarded sessions in the global Apple developer community. We're excited to bring these world-class perspectives to Taiwan.

Our first speaker is Rudrank Riyam, who was recently recognized as an Apple Community Contributor. Since joining the Apple developer ecosystem in 2018, Rudrank has published tutorials covering MusicKit, MLX Swift, Foundation Models, and other cutting-edge frameworks. He is also the maintainer of popular open-source projects such as MusadoraKit and ASC CLI. As an active member of the global iOS community and one of the organizers behind the AiOS meetup in Cupertino following WWDC, Rudrank will present "For Agents, By Agents: Building AI Tools That Maintain Themselves," sharing how AI agents can participate in the development, maintenance, and evolution of open-source tools.

Our second speaker, Minkyung Kim, is the Learning Lead at Apple Developer Academy @ POSTECH and an EdTech specialist whose work bridges Computer Science and Education. Her research focuses on how people learn and how technology can support that process. In her session, "Designing Adaptive UX Through Learning Theory & AI," she will explore how concepts such as Cognitive Load Theory, the Zone of Proximal Development (ZPD), and Flow Theory can be applied to product design. She will also demonstrate how On-Device AI can enable adaptive user experiences that understand user progress and dynamically adjust interactions in real time.

Our third speaker is JungMin Ahn, a Senior Software Engineer at KakaoBank with more than 13 years of iOS development experience. He specializes in large-scale architecture, developer productivity, and long-term maintainability. In his session, "Lessons from Operating Large-Scale Modular iOS Projects with Tuist," he will share practical lessons from managing large modularized iOS codebases, covering project file management, dependency organization, testing strategies, team collaboration, and how to prevent architecture from becoming increasingly difficult to maintain as projects grow.

In addition, tickets for iPlayground 2026 are now available, and our Call for Papers is officially open until June 19. This year, we are introducing a new 5-minute Lightning Talk format to lower the barrier for first-time speakers and encourage more community participation. We warmly invite you to submit a session proposal and share your experience, insights, and stories with the community. After all, iPlayground only happens once a year.

title: Recommend a Sponsor and Get a Free iPlayground Ticket!
content:
iPlayground 2026 will take place on July 25–26 at NCCU CPBAE, bringing together 300+ iOS engineers, product builders, and designers. As Taiwan's annual showcase for the iOS community, this year's event will feature well-known local developers, KOLs, invited speakers from international conferences, and community activities designed to bring people together.

To make all of this happen, we are looking for sponsor referrals from friends across the community. From now until May 22, if you recommend a company contact and the introduction leads to a sponsorship conversation and partnership, we will thank you with one two-day iPlayground 2026 ticket, valued at NT$4,000, including one commemorative shirt. If you have ideas for sponsorship collaboration, please leave your information and we will get in touch soon.
